intro.uz: "Break Rules" — qoidalarni buzish. AJ Hoge (Seth Godin kitobidan) aytadiki, eski, anʼanaviy usullarni buzgan kishigina ajralib turadi va tez oʻrganadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi ayni shu ruhda: zidlik ergash gaplari (although / even though / despite / whereas) — bir faktni tan olib, unga qarshi chiqish; va urgʻu uchun inversiya (Never have I…; Not only… but also…) — SVO qoidasini ataylab buzib, kuchli taʼkid berish.
intro.en: "Break Rules" — AJ Hoge (from Seth Godin's book) says only the person who breaks the old, traditional methods stands out and learns fast. This week's two grammar topics match that spirit: concession clauses (although / even though / despite / whereas) — conceding one fact and pushing against it; and inversion for emphasis (Never have I…; Not only… but also…) — deliberately breaking the SVO rule for effect.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida although / despite / whereas yordamida ikki qarama-qarshi fikrni bitta jumlada bogʻlay olasiz va urgʻu berish uchun "Never have I…", "Not only… but also…" kabi inversiya jumlalarini toʻgʻri qura olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can join two contrasting ideas in one sentence with although / despite / whereas, and correctly form emphatic inversions like "Never have I…" and "Not only… but also…".

grammar.titleUz: Zidlik ergash gaplari: although / even though / despite / whereas
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Writing Task 2 — concede a counter-argument, then push back on it
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can concede a point and contrast it inside one complex sentence
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tili zidlikni feʼlga qoʻshiladigan "-sa ham / -ga qaramay" bilan beradi: "charchagan boʻlsam ham". Ingliz tilida esa alohida bogʻlovchi jumla boshida turadi: although / even though + toʻliq gap (ega + feʼl), despite / in spite of + ot yoki -ing. Bu farqni oʻzbekcha bitta qoʻshimcha yashiradi — shuning uchun ikkisini adashtirish oson.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ Despite it was late, we stayed. ❌ Although the late hour, we stayed. ✅ Although it was late, we stayed. ✅ Despite the late hour, we stayed. although/even though — toʻliq gap bilan; despite/in spite of — ot yoki -ing bilan (❌ despite of).

examples.en: Although school taught us grammar, we learned to speak by listening.
examples.uz: Maktab bizga grammatika oʻrgatgan boʻlsa-da, biz gapirishni tinglab oʻrgandik.

examples.en: Despite his fear, he broke the rules.
A factory needs obedience, whereas an office needs ideas.
examples.uz: Zavod itoatni talab qiladi, ofis esa gʻoyani.

exercises.prompt: ___ it was raining, we went outside.
exercises.hintUz: keyin toʻliq gap (it was raining) → although
keyin ot (the rain) → despite / in spite of

exercises.prompt: He loves risk, ___ his brother avoids it.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda bir fikrni ikki xil ayting (ovoz chiqarib): avval "Although I …, I …", keyin "Despite …, I …".
exercises.hintUz: although + toʻliq gap; despite + ot yoki -ing

grammar.titleUz: Inversiya (urgʻu): Never have I…; Not only… but also…
grammar.titleEn: Inversion for emphasis — Never have I…; Not only…
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — a B2+ flourish that makes one point stand out
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2+: I can front a negative adverbial and invert the auxiliary for emphasis
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tili urgʻuni soʻz tartibini biroz oʻzgartirib yoki "hech qachon / nafaqat" soʻzlari bilan beradi, lekin feʼlning oʻrni oʻzgarmaydi. Ingliz tilida esa inkor soʻz gap boshiga chiqsa, yordamchi feʼl (have / do / did) egadan OLDIN keladi — xuddi savoldek, lekin bu savol emas, kuchli taʼkid.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ Never I have seen this. ❌ Not only he broke the rule. ✅ Never have I seen this. ✅ Not only did he break the rule (but he also won). Inkor soʻz oldinga chiqsa — yordamchi feʼl egadan oldin turadi, asosiy feʼl esa sof qoladi.

examples.en: Never have I learned English so fast.
examples.uz: Hech qachon ingliz tilini bunchalik tez oʻrganmaganman.

examples.en: Not only did he break the rules, but he also succeeded.
examples.uz: U nafaqat qoidalarni buzdi, balki muvaffaqiyatga ham erishdi.

examples.en: Little did I know that breaking rules would change my life.
examples.uz: Qoidalarni buzish hayotimni oʻzgartirishini sira bilmasdim.

exercises.prompt: Never ___ I seen such energy in a class.
exercises.hintUz: never + present perfect → have + ega (I have seen)

exercises.prompt: Not only ___ she break the rule, but she also won.
exercises.hintUz: Not only oldinga chiqsa → did + ega + sof feʼl (break)

exercises.prompt: Little did I ___ how much it would help.
exercises.hintUz: Little did I + sof feʼl (know) — did allaqachon oʻtmishni bildiradi

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda kuchli bir jumla ayting (ovoz chiqarib): "Never have I …" yoki "Not only … but also …".
exercises.hintUz: yordamchi feʼlni (have / did) egadan oldin qoʻying

vocab.defEn: the act of following the rules or doing what you are told
vocab.example: Obedience works fine in a standardized factory.

vocab.uz: bir xil andozadagi test (hamma uchun bir xil)
vocab.defEn: a test that is always exactly the same for everyone
vocab.example: A standardized test measures everyone the same way.

vocab.defEn: a factory or manual job where you use your hands
vocab.example: A blue-collar job usually means working in a factory.

vocab.defEn: an office or mental job where you use your brain
vocab.example: White-collar workers are paid to use their heads.

vocab.uz: unumdorlik, samaradorlik (kam kuch bilan koʻp ish)
vocab.defEn: how much work you do in a certain time or with a certain effort
to be extremely scared or afraid
vocab.example: Most workers are petrified to break the rules.

vocab.defEn: to fail or make a big mistake
vocab.example: We don't want to blow it at work.
You need to invent new instructions, not just follow old ones.

vocab.defEn: a story or belief that is not true
vocab.example: It's a myth that following orders keeps your job safe.

vocab.defEn: a fast, automatic reaction with no thinking
vocab.example: His reflex was to ask the boss what to do.

englishpod.title: The Office – Asking About the Position

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebecca jurnalistlik ishiga suhbatga keladi va xodimlar soni, ish vaqti hamda boʻsh oʻrin haqida dadil savollar beradi. AJ aytadiki, ofis xodimi qoidalarni buzib, oʻzi mustaqil fikrlashi kerak — hatto suhbatda ham botir savol bergan kishi ajralib turadi. Sizningcha, Rebecca ishga qabul qilinadimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca interviews for a journalism job and asks bold questions about the workforce, the hours and the vacancy. AJ says white-collar workers should break the rules and think for themselves — even in an interview, the person who asks confident questions stands out. Do you think Rebecca will get the job?

keyVocab.defEn: a way to introduce or ask about a new topic
all the people who work for a company
the group of people who work for an organization
available to work at any time when needed
to hire someone for an empty position

quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) bread. 'Money' iborasi 'bread and honey' bilan qofiyalanadi, ammo kokni slengida faqat birinchi soʻz — 'bread' — ishlatiladi. Neil uch variant berdi (bread, honey, dough), Alice 'bread' dedi va haqli chiqdi.

vocab.defEn: informal language spoken by a particular group
rude language that offends or upsets people
the technical language belonging to a specific group

funEnglish.title: Why Breaking the Rules Can Make You Successful

speakingPrompt.en: Think about a time you broke a rule — at school, at work, or in learning English. Although following rules feels safe, why did breaking this one help you? Not only say what you did, but also what you learned.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: zidlik ergash gaplari (although / even though / despite / whereas) va urgʻu uchun inversiya (Never have I…; Not only… but also…).
